title: cakile lacustris
intro: Cakile lacustris, also known as seabeach sandwort, is a perennial flowering plant that grows in coastal areas, particularly in sandy or gravelly soils. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cakile lacustris include reducing inflammation, fighting bacterial infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Cakile lacustris can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used topically to treat skin irritations and injuries.
